123816.explanation: This initially does not seem desirable for a sports hall, but for managing a public garden in your street or neighborhood we see possibilities.

123820.explanation: There are enough here at Ekkersrijt; elsewhere within the municipality border there is no space.

123821.explanation: Creating an asylum seekers center is not a goal; we have to comply with a distribution law and thus national regulations.

123824.explanation: Education about their usage should receive attention; burn responsibly.

123835.explanation: If this were already the case, it would already be happening!

123838.explanation: Even better; being visible, easily approachable, and more accessible for residents to promote a feeling of safety.

123839.explanation: This should be possible under strict conditions.

123840.explanation: You should not speak about this publicly; it concerns very vulnerable people, it may already exist. Municipalities have a responsibility here, this can be done individually or jointly with, for example, a neighboring municipality.

123842.explanation: Already exists, within regional agreements. Allocating 100% is undesirable, otherwise someone could never qualify for a rental home in another municipality if every municipality would follow this policy.

123846.explanation: Sounds sympathetic and it is important that everyone can continue to participate. Mandatory measures are not always effective or possible. We can look at whether and how we can stimulate this.

123848.explanation: Anyone who needs help must be able to get it; an income limit is not always the right tool. We support targeted assistance. Here too, we will search together for a structural solution.

123850.explanation: The current occupancy and locations must be reviewed; adjustments must be made depending on demand and supply. This can involve adding or relocating charging stations.

123853.explanation: Price should never be the only criterion. Especially in youth care, where lessons from the past show this approach is wrong.

123854.explanation: Municipal expenditures must be structurally covered by revenues; the property tax is part of the municipality's income stream. Its amount correlates with the municipality's ambitions. The more new (extra) ambitions, the more money is needed...

123858.explanation: This is national policy! Municipalities have no say, they receive allocated numbers from the Province and the national government.

123859.explanation: The polluter pays; otherwise, waste disposal charges rise for everyone.

123865.explanation: We see residential towers as large-scale urban developments. We find these inappropriate. Depending on location, architecture, and spatial integration, we do see possibilities for apartment buildings of about 4 floors due to feasibility and affordability.

123867.explanation: Every centenarian deserves a tree and in addition, we want an annual planting for every newborn resident, in the form of a birth forest. This way we continue investing in a green municipality with attention to its residents. See our election program on this theme.

123868.explanation: The municipality already does this, research is underway.

123869.explanation: We must support and help residents who struggle to keep up. Additionally, this measure proved ineffective in the past.

123816.explanation: The CDA believes in the strength of our community. If residents or associations can manage a sports hall or public garden well, safely, and affordably, the municipality should provide space and support. Bottom-up initiatives deserve trust.

123820.explanation: Within the MRE region, agreements have been made to carefully utilize space for large distribution centers. Son en Breugel cannot accommodate more large distribution centers within these considerations. Therefore, the municipality retains control over possible locations.

123821.explanation: The CDA stands for solidarity and a reliable government. We must follow applicable legislation, including agreements on asylum seeker reception. How this is implemented locally requires care, support, and a humane approach that suits Son en Breugel.

123824.explanation: The CDA believes that residents should be able to heat their homes, but also cares about health and air quality. The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves requires caution. We focus on education, reducing nuisance, and cleaner alternatives, rather than a total ban.

123829.explanation: The CDA values accessibility and wants everyone to be able to participate. Entrepreneurs are responsible themselves, but the municipality is happy to think along and support wherever possible with advice, knowledge, or by connecting to schemes.

123834.explanation: The CDA considers music education important for children's development. We have agreed to safeguard accessible music education, with support targeted to families who need it. This way music lessons remain accessible to every child, fairly.

123835.explanation: The CDA sees sports clubs as very valuable, but our financial resources are not unlimited. We ensure a clear and fair subsidy policy, with indexed subsidies and clear accommodation policies so clubs know what to expect and can plan for the future.

123838.explanation: The CDA finds safety important and opts for a smart approach. Safety is more than just enforcement. Not the number of enforcement officers matters most, but effective deployment, cooperation with the police, and attention to prevention. This keeps Son en Breugel safe and livable.

123839.explanation: The CDA regards safety as important. Camera surveillance can help with this, such as at the Ekkersrijt industrial area where it yields good results. We deploy this selectively, mainly at industrial areas and locations where it contributes to safety and crime prevention.

123840.explanation: The CDA considers good shelter for domestic violence victims essential. For a small municipality like Son en Breugel, having its own women's shelter is not appropriate. We therefore collaborate regionally, so victims receive professional and safe care when needed.

123842.explanation: The CDA believes that residents of Son en Breugel should be able to continue living in our village as much as possible. We adhere to regional rules, but with new housing projects we talk with developers to allocate a portion of homes to people from Son en Breugel.

123846.explanation: The CDA values participation. We expect effort from people on social assistance, but not everyone can manage this. We choose customized solutions: those who can do socially useful work or other appropriate activities, with guidance and consideration of their personal situation.

123848.explanation: The CDA has long supported residents with low incomes in Son en Breugel. The income threshold was recently raised and there are many good income-related schemes. Our focus now is mainly on better and clear information, so people know what help is available and can actually use it.

123850.explanation: The CDA wants to encourage sustainable transport. Electric driving fits in with this. Therefore, we ensure sufficient public charging points in Son en Breugel with an established charging plan. Charging points at homes are not always exclusive to electric cars.

123853.explanation: The CDA considers good youth care important. In procurement, we collaborate regionally through a fixed arrangement. This way we look not only at price but especially at quality and appropriate help. By working together, we keep costs manageable and care effective.

123854.explanation: The CDA believes that housing costs for residents and entrepreneurs must remain affordable. We want to handle public funds carefully and do not simply choose higher taxes. Only when really necessary do we make a responsible trade-off.

123858.explanation: The CDA finds it important that status holders are housed as legally required. At the same time, we want housing seekers from Son en Breugel to be treated fairly. Within regional and legal agreements, we seek a balanced distribution.

123859.explanation: The CDA wants waste to be managed well, fairly, and affordably. With the new, accessible recycling center, clear rules have been introduced. Since January 1, a trunk rate applies, meaning small amounts of waste cost less. We choose an affordable and careful system.

123860.explanation: The CDA wants sustainable energy but also to use agricultural land and the landscape sparingly. First, we use roofs for solar panels. We consider each location carefully to determine what is wise and appropriate.

123865.explanation: The CDA wants to preserve the village character of Son en Breugel. Because space is limited, some places may allow slightly taller buildings. We always do this in harmony with the surroundings, with a clear limit on the number of floors and without very tall tower blocks.

123867.explanation: The CDA considers it important to honor residents who turn 100 in an appropriate way. Son en Breugel is a green municipality with sufficient possibilities. We continue to look for suitable locations and other ways to celebrate this special milestone.

123868.explanation: The CDA believes that building in areas with noise pollution can only take place after very careful consideration. We base decisions on current knowledge and carefully weigh health, nature, and the living environment.

123869.explanation: The CDA believes that people with low incomes should have access to the internet. The previous scheme was discontinued against our wishes. It was promised that the cutback would benefit this group in another way. We will continue to strive to make this happen.

123816.explanation: If this can be done in a way that is good for everyone, we are open to it.

123820.explanation: Son en Breugel (Ekkersrijt) is located at a highway junction, so the establishment of distribution centers there is not directly a problem.

123821.explanation: We want to limit the influx of asylum seekers and believe the existing asylum centers should be sufficient for housing.

123824.explanation: We agree with this, provided residents use it responsibly.

123829.explanation: This can be arranged well by the entrepreneur themselves, as included in the regulations.

123834.explanation: We must support the children of people who cannot afford this themselves.

123835.explanation: The clubs must ensure a healthy financial administration themselves. The municipality often already supports them with accommodations.

123838.explanation: We truly value safety and often hear from residents about unsafe situations. Therefore, we must focus on sufficient enforcement officers.

123839.explanation: There is regular vandalism. Perhaps camera surveillance at certain locations is an option.

123840.explanation: We are too small for such initiatives and it is better to address these regionally.

123846.explanation: It is good to reintegrate people from social assistance into the labor process through socially useful work.

123848.explanation: This is arranged nationally; as a municipality we have no role in this.

123850.explanation: We have already invested well in charging stations and now a clear policy on costs must be developed.

123853.explanation: Good care but within financial limits.

123854.explanation: The municipality must adjust spending to income and not keep raising taxes for residents.

123858.explanation: We stand for equitable distribution of available housing.

123859.explanation: That would be ideal, but distinguishing private from business waste makes that difficult.

123860.explanation: Here too, this can and must be possible in some areas, naturally within the considerations included in the environmental vision.

123865.explanation: Of course, a definition of what is considered tall is needed. We currently have a few residential towers of 5 floors next to the canal. Perhaps there are possibilities in more locations.

123867.explanation: Planting trees is a good option, but placing a bench, as has been done recently, is also a nice solution.

123868.explanation: We need to look at the possibilities and be innovative, not just focus on what is not possible.

123869.explanation: We must provide everyone access to the digital highway in order to function well socially.

123816.explanation: GL/PvdA supports own initiatives from village residents who are able and willing to take them.

123820.explanation: GL/PvdA strives for high-quality employment and wants to prevent landscape pollution.

123821.explanation: Consistent with the municipality's approach and our community's capacity, we contribute to humane reception of refugees.

123824.explanation: A ban in Son en Breugel goes too far for GL/PvdA at this time. However, we do support good information about responsible wood burning and the establishment of a complaints procedure in case of nuisance.

123829.explanation: Accessibility is essential for an inclusive community where everyone has the chance to participate.

123834.explanation: Income dependency is the basic principle for GL/PvdA. People contribute according to their capacity.

123838.explanation: Ensure sufficient safety in the village with the current BOAS officers and neighborhood police officers.

123839.explanation: Privacy of residents is a great asset. However, GL/PvdA wants to be able to make exceptions for places that are unsafe for women or where the safety of other groups is at risk.

123840.explanation: Such an important facility is preferably realized in cooperation with other municipalities in the region.

123842.explanation: Current priority arrangements for, among others, women in unsafe positions will be continued.

123846.explanation: The government does not regulate how people find meaning in their lives. Solidarity is unconditional.

123848.explanation: Maintenance costs are rising sharply. GL/PvdA prevents people from living in poverty. PvdA/GL applies a generous standard for people living around the social minimum.

123850.explanation: GL/PvdA aims for a climate-neutral village.

123854.explanation: Compared to surrounding municipalities, Son en Breugel has a low property tax (OZB). Given the anticipated financial challenges in areas such as youth care and maintaining local facilities, aligning the OZB with other municipalities is negotiable.

123858.explanation: In line with current policy. This only applies up to an extra month of waiting time for social housing.

123860.explanation: GL/PvdA sees small-scale initiatives to generate solar energy as a way to become a climate-neutral village.

123865.explanation: The village character of Son en Breugel is a great asset. The current housing task can be achieved without high residential towers.

123867.explanation: GL/PvdA finds this a sympathetic initiative.

123868.explanation: This aligns with the municipality's current policy, which includes working with the airport to counter noise nuisance.

123869.explanation: Access to the internet is a prerequisite for participation in society.

123816.explanation: Under certain conditions, D66 supports transferring management. Of course, the municipality remains responsible and in control, especially regarding finances.

123820.explanation: D66 focuses on the region's high-tech sector. Further expansion of distribution centers in Son en Breugel is not an option for D66.

123821.explanation: D66 supports the Distribution Act and believes that Son en Breugel must also contribute its share when necessary.

123824.explanation: D66 believes people should be better informed about the harmful effects of wood burning on health and how to prevent nuisance, rather than imposing an immediate ban.

123829.explanation: D66 emphasizes equal opportunities and full participation in society for people with disabilities. Therefore, D66 advocates for a local inclusion agenda to improve accessibility. Entrepreneurs also have their own responsibilities. The municipality can support this.

123834.explanation: D66 stands for accessible music education for every child and advocates for a broader subsidy scheme.

123835.explanation: D66 is pleased with the wide range of sports clubs in Son en Breugel and finds it important that this continues. D66 is positive about financially supporting sports clubs and stresses the importance of a healthy and accessible sports climate for everyone.

123838.explanation: D66 supports a safe living environment, and municipal enforcement officers currently make a sufficient contribution in Son en Breugel.

123839.explanation: D66 takes a cautious stance regarding the use of camera surveillance in Son en Breugel. D66 emphasizes citizens' privacy and believes camera surveillance should only be used under strict conditions.

123840.explanation: The municipality of Son en Breugel has made regional agreements on this. Expanding the availability of women's shelters must be addressed regionally.

123842.explanation: D66 believes that in some cases priority can be given to home seekers from the municipality itself, such as tenants affected by neighborhood renewal or urgent cases. Custom solutions must be possible. D66 opposes a generic priority scheme.

123846.explanation: D66 supports participation according to capacity and encourages everyone to contribute to a social society. D66 believes tailored approaches work much better. The municipality has a supporting and guiding role.

123848.explanation: Existential security is important for D66. The income threshold is already adjusted annually for inflation. Additionally, Special Assistance, Social Support Act (WMO), and Youth Act provisions remain available to everyone. Access to help is well organized with the CMD in Son en Breugel.

123850.explanation: D66 believes that when the demand for charging points grows, the supply must grow accordingly. This is already policy in Son en Breugel. Sustainable mobility is a spearhead for D66.

123853.explanation: Due to large shortages in youth care, controlling costs is very important. D66 believes the quality of care is equally important in achieving effective and sustainable solutions.

123854.explanation: D66 believes that the property tax (OZB) should not be increased. First and foremost, D66 supports more housing construction to generate additional income. Furthermore, D66 is of the opinion that the national government contribution should fully cover the legal tasks that the municipality must perform.

123858.explanation: D66 supports limiting the inflow and ensuring a proper flow through the asylum process. Currently, it is necessary that refugees with a residence permit are given priority. D66 wants many more social rental homes so that there is suitable housing available for everyone.

123859.explanation: D66 aims to make free access to the recycling center possible four times a year. D66 sees this as an important service for residents and it helps prevent illegal dumping of waste. Completely free waste disposal is financially not feasible.

123860.explanation: D66 supports generating sustainable energy, especially in areas where housing construction is not possible. This requires municipal coordination to prevent large solar parks from being built everywhere. Preservation of nature and cultural landscapes must be taken into account.

123865.explanation: D66 is not against high-rise buildings. These increase the supply of homes. However, they must fit within the environment. The parking standards must be maintained.

123867.explanation: D66 thinks this is a nice initiative and would like to restore it. D66 believes there are sufficient places in Son en Breugel to plant new trees.

123868.explanation: D66 finds it undesirable to build homes that will be subject to excessive noise nuisance from Eindhoven Airport. Moreover, it is important that there is no increase in flights from Eindhoven Airport and that efforts are made to reduce noise.

123869.explanation: D66 strives for digital inclusion and simultaneously believes that internet costs are part of a household’s basic expenses. Residents have their own responsibility in this. Where necessary, tailored support can be provided through existing income schemes.

123816.explanation: It is complicated; regarding maintenance that involves costs, municipal input is necessary. It is great when residents help, but in Son en Breugel this already happens a lot due to many volunteers.

123820.explanation: If Ekkersrijt has opportunities here, they should definitely be explored. It contributes to employment, including for the residents of Son en Breugel.

123821.explanation: We do not want to be a refusal municipality; additionally, the distribution law is still in force, so it is better to retain control ourselves than to have a location assigned by the government.

123824.explanation: They are polluters and residents with lung problems suffer serious discomfort from this.

123829.explanation: A subsidy could be applied for.

123834.explanation: Some parents can pay for it themselves; for those who cannot, there is a fund available.

123835.explanation: Sports clubs receive a lot of subsidies and are helped when there are extra costs.

123838.explanation: There are really too few of them now.

123839.explanation: In the more unsafe places where people, including children, pass by in the evening.

123840.explanation: Women must be able to find their way easily when facing domestic violence.

123842.explanation: By now yes, there is such a large housing shortage that even our children and grandchildren should have a chance to remain in their village.

123848.explanation: Yes, that may be increased to 130% of the social assistance standard.

123850.explanation: More and more electric cars are coming, and we must adjust the charging points accordingly.

123853.explanation: Fortunately, fixed amounts have now been established by the national government, providing a reliable basis.

123854.explanation: If we want to maintain all facilities and services in Son en Breugel at the current level, that is necessary.

123858.explanation: The law requires that a number of status holders be placed per municipality, which sometimes makes it seem they get priority over people who are registered. There is currently no other solution available.

123859.explanation: Some items yes, such as green waste and styrofoam—items that you cannot and should not throw in with regular waste. It is important to maintain standard guidelines and pricing.

123860.explanation: We are not supporters of solar fields. First, fill all the roofs with solar panels.

123865.explanation: Three residential floors is our maximum in neighborhoods near water, possibly four floors with an extension up to six.

123867.explanation: We have no opinion on this.

123868.explanation: It simply cannot be. The number of allowable DB (displaced persons) for people is fixed.

123869.explanation: Internet must be and remain accessible to everyone.

statements.title: Son en Breugel should investigate whether it can still build in the area affected by noise from Eindhoven Airport.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Son en Breugel.

titleParts.text: In Son en Breugel moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Son en Breugel.

titleParts.text: In Son en Breugel moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should cover internet costs for people with a low income.
More high-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Son en Breugel.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: A women's shelter (domestic violence refuge) should be established in Son en Breugel.

titleParts.text: In Son en Breugel moet een

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: There should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Son en Breugel who focus on safety.

titleParts.text: In Son en Breugel moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should still look for a new location to plant trees for 100-year-olds.
